import io
import string
import list

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
end

fn next_token(_ s: String, _ start: Int) -> List<Int>
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = start
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if is_ws(b)
        i = i + 1
      else
        i = n + 1
      end
    when None
      i = n
    end
  end
  if i > n
    let begin = i - 1
    mut j = begin
    mut done = false
    while j < n and not done
      match string.byte_at(s, j)
      when Some(b)
        if is_ws(b)
          done = true
        else
          j = j + 1
        end
      when None
        done = true
      end
    end
    [begin, j]
  else
    [-1, -1]
  end
end

fn parse_three(_ s: String) -> Option<List<Int>>
  let t1 = next_token(s, 0)
  let s1 = t1[0]
  let e1 = t1[1]
  if s1 < 0
    None
  else
    let t2 = next_token(s, e1)
    let s2 = t2[0]
    let e2 = t2[1]
    if s2 < 0
      None
    else
      let t3 = next_token(s, e2)
      let s3 = t3[0]
      let e3 = t3[1]
      if s3 < 0
        None
      else
        let a_str = string.substring(s, s1, e1)
        let b_str = string.substring(s, s2, e2)
        let s_str = string.substring(s, s3, e3)
        match string.parse_int(a_str)
        when Some(a)
          match string.parse_int(b_str)
          when Some(b)
            match string.parse_int(s_str)
            when Some(st)
              Some([a, b, st])
            when None
              None
            end
          when None
            None
          end
        when None
          None
        end
      end
    end
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let input = io.read_to_end()
  match parse_three(input)
  when Some(vals)
    let a = vals[0]
    let b = vals[1]
    let s = vals[2]
    mut out = ""
    mut x = a
    mut first = true
    while x < b
      if first
        out = "#{x}"
        first = false
      else
        out = "#{out} #{x}"
      end
      x = x + s
    end
    println(out)
  when None
    println("")
  end
end
